issues.title: Bug: ConveyerController.initialise() is dead code in restart.gd — called after reload_current_scene()
issues.body:
## Bug Description

In `restart.gd`, the `_on_button_pressed()` function calls `ConveyerController.initialise()` **after** `get_tree().reload_current_scene()`. Since `reload_current_scene()` triggers a full scene tree teardown and rebuild, the `ConveyerController.initialise()` call on line 19 is effectively **dead code** — it never executes in the expected context.

## Location

`Scripts/restart.gd` — lines 13–19

```gdscript
func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	print("restart clicked")
	Level.initialise()
	get_tree().reload_current_scene()   # scene reloads here
	ConveyerController.initialise()     # ← never executes properly

issues.title: Bug: Multiple Conveyor belts forms  b/w sink and broker when a single sink is clicked multiple times.
Multi-sink level sometimes shows failure result even on success
issues.body: In the multi-sink level, the level result incorrectly shows "failure" sometimes, even when the player has succeeded.

issues.title: Inconsistent game object setup makes property changes error-prone and time-consuming.
issues.body:
Game objects across the project are instantiated and managed inconsistently. The same type of object may be:
- Present as a local node in the scene tree
- Loaded as a saved/inherited scene(like prefabs in unity)

This inconsistency makes the codebase harder to maintain, debug, and extend.

I believe if the same object is loaded as inherited scene for the different level (if possible ,depending on the level), would be better. So it is easier to maintain the game and if we want to have a change in a specific game object property we don't have to change it manually in every scene.

issues.title: Disable START button until a route is connected
issues.body: START button should be disabled and grayed out until the player connects at least one box to a sink. This prevents accidental clicks and guides the player to complete the routing step first.

issues.title: UI Polishing/Popup Redesign
issues.body:
I've noticed that on  success/failure popups didn't really match they currently feel out of place.

TASK:-
Better visuals popups: Give the popups a redesign

issues.title: Add toast notifications for game actions
issues.body: Add a toast notification system that displays brief popup messages for key game events like starting, routing, success, and failure. Improves user feedback and game polish.

issues.title: Add interactive visual tutorial for level 1 onboarding
issues.body:
##Problem
Currently, the onboarding experience for new players relies on static text or mental models of Knative eventing.

This feature introduces a guided interaction system to help players understand the flow.
"Source -> Trigger -> Sink" flow intuitively.

issues.title: Add In-Game Concept Glossary & Help Button
issues.body:
## Problem
Players see terms like "DLQ" but don't know what they mean. They need a way to look up concepts without leaving the game.

## Solution
Add a **Concept Glossary** that explains Knative terms.

**Features:**
**•Searchable Dictionary:** 10+ terms (Broker, Trigger, Source, etc.)
**•Game vs Real World:** Explains how the game mechanic relates to real Knative.
**•Global Help Button:** A `?` button on the top-right of every level.
**•Easy Access:** Works anytime (success or failure).

This makes the game a proper learning tool, not just a puzzle!

issues.title: Add Level Selection Menu for Better Navigation
issues.body:
## Problem
Players must complete levels sequentially. Cannot jump to specific patterns they want to learn or replay completed levels.

## Proposed Solution
Create a main menu screen showing all 4 levels:
- Visual cards for each level
- Lock/unlock status based on completion
- Brief description of what pattern each teaches
- "Play" button to launch level
- Progress indicators

## Benefits
- Improved user experience and navigation
- Allows targeted learning (jump to specific pattern)
- Shows player progress visually
- Professional game standard feature

## Implementation Approach
- New scene: `main_menu.tscn`
- Save/load completion status with ConfigFile
- Level cards with thumbnails and descriptions
- Unlock levels based on previous completion

I'm willing to implement this!

issues.title: Add pre-level introduction screen to explain EDA game objects
issues.body:
Currently, players are directly taken into Level 1 without any prior explanation of the core Event Driven Architecture (EDA) concepts used in the game.

This can cause confusion for first-time players, as objects such as:

- Events
- Filters
- Sinks
- Transformers
- Dead Letter Queue (DLQ)

are introduced only during gameplay, without prior context.

issues.title: keyboard shortcuts for accessibility and faster gameplay.

Add keyboard shortcuts for accessibility and faster gameplay.

## Shortcuts

- Esc = Quit / Go to Level 1

## Files

issues.title: Add optional GDScript linting setup for improved code quality
issues.body:
The project currently does not include a standardized linting setup for GDScript, which can result in inconsistent formatting and reduced readability across contributions.

As the codebase grows and more contributors get involved, having a lightweight and optional linting mechanism can significantly improve maintainability.

Problem

- No consistent GDScript style enforcement
- Harder to review PRs due to formatting differences
- Contributors may unknowingly introduce style issues

issues.title: Add Interactive Tutorial & Learning System
issues.body:
**Problem:-**
New players open the game and have no idea what to do. When they fail, they just see "Failed. Try Again" with zero explanation.

The game has good mechanics but do not actually teach Knative concepts. Other PRs (https://github.com/knative-extensions/educational-game/pull/68, https://github.com/knative-extensions/educational-game/pull/70, https://github.com/knative-extensions/educational-game/pull/73, https://github.com/knative-extensions/educational-game/pull/76) are adding UI , but nobody's building the teaching layer.

Add a tutorial system that guides players and explains failures:-

Tutorial tooltips:
"Welcome! In Knative, events flow from Sources to Sinks"
"Click this event to select it"
"Great! You just created a basic event flow"
Smart failure messages:

Why:- Wrong event type delivered to sink
Hint: Use filters to match colors
Knative lesson: Triggers filter events by attributes

## Summary

## Problem

issues.title: Display current EDA pattern name during gameplay
issues.body:
Currently, learners are not explicitly informed about which Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) pattern a level represents while playing the game.

This issue proposes displaying the current pattern name (for example: "Pattern: Basic Event Flow" or "Pattern: Dead Letter Queue") during gameplay.

Showing the pattern name helps learners better understand the architectural concept being demonstrated in each level and improves learning clarity without affecting gameplay logic.

issues.title: Add level progress indicator to show learner progression across levels
issues.body:
Currently, the game does not provide a clear indication of how many levels a learner has completed or how many remain.
This issue proposes adding a simple level progress indicator displayed at the top-left corner of the screen in the format:

`Level Progress: 1 / 3`

The indicator updates automatically as the learner completes each level, helping users better understand their progression through the Event-Driven Architecture patterns without introducing gamified point-based scoring.
This improvement focuses on learning clarity and user orientation, especially for first-time learners navigating multiple EDA concepts.

issues.title: Add sink availability indicator
issues.body:
Add a blinking light to the sink that indicates whether the sink is up or down. 
Also, introduce an "available or up/down" property /export var on the sink. 
- The blinking light should be active only when the sink is available.
- Make sure the default state of the sink is available (up).
- You don't need to take care of the whole implementation of the unavailable sink like retry mechanism and DLS, but  just make   
   sure the light goes off on the sink when its in down state.

issues.title: Application crashes when creating invalid or incomplete event connections
issues.body:
While testing different levels, multiple crash scenarios were observed related to invalid or incomplete event flow setups.

The application crashes in the following cases:
- When attempting to connect a funnel (event source) directly to a sink, especially after a valid Trigger → Sink connection is already established.
- When users create incomplete or partially connected event paths.
- When the simulation is started before all required connections are properly configured.
- When internal conveyor and destination arrays become out of sync, leading to unsafe array access.

Ideally, the system should not crash under any user interaction. Instead, it should either prevent unsupported connections or handle such cases gracefully.

Examples: 


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b5786c1-cbc9-483b-aaa7-9dd550671d14


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad0bcb02-5425-4a8f-bae1-c75135c25fe7

issues.title: no
[Bug] Game crashes with "Invalid access to index" on double-clicking the Sink
issues.body:
Description: While testing the basicEventFlow scene locally, I noticed that the game crashes if the user clicks the Sink button multiple times (or double-clicks) after selecting a box.

Steps to Reproduce:

1-Run the basicEventFlow scene.

2-Click on the Blue Box to select it.

3-Click on the Gray Sink container twice (double-click).

Expected Behavior The box should move to the sink, and subsequent clicks should be ignored if no conveyor is available.

Actual Behavior The game crashes with the error: Invalid access to index 1 on a base object of type: 'Array'.

Reason In ConveyerController.gd, the conveyer array has a size of 1. A second click increments conveyerInd to 1, causing an out-of-bounds access when conveyer[conveyerInd] is called.

Proposed Fix I have fixed this locally by adding a safety check in the transfer_box function to ensure conveyerInd does not exceed the array size.

Would you like me to raise a PR for this fix?

The repository currently lacks a README.md file, making it difficult for new contributors and users to understand:

## Proposed Solution

- Project title and description
- Overview of EDA concepts taught in the game

issues.title: Design and Implement Levels
issues.body:
## Overview

Currently, the game is a prototype that shows the concept but is not a playable educational experience. In discussions with @evankanderson there was a suggestion to add levels to the game which ask the users to solve puzzles, teaching architectural concepts. Specifically, these levels would consist of services which can transform the shape/colour of an event, as well as brokers, triggers, filters, sources, and sinks. The goal of the player would be to successfully connect the sources to sinks with any required intermediate services.

This is a parent issue for a lot of work, but in the scope is:
- [ ] Design levels that teach architectural concepts
- [ ] Generalize existing prototype code to support levels
- [ ] Implement levels into the game

NOTE: for anyone applying to this project through the LFX mentorship program, please see https://github.com/knative-extensions/educational-game/issues/8#issuecomment-2637993619

pullRequests.body:
fix: move ConveyerController initialisation before scene reload

Closes #107

Right now when you press the restart button:
1.  First the entire scene gets reloaded and destroyed
2.  Then the code tries to reset the ConveyerController which doesn't exist anymore

That second line was completely useless dead code that never ran properly.

This just swaps those two lines so we reset all state first, then reload the level with clean initialised values.

Simple one line fix, no breaking changes.

pullRequests.body:
## Summary
Adds a toast notification system to provide visual feedback for game actions.

## Changes
- Created `ToastManager.gd` autoload for managing toast notifications
- Created `toast.tscn` and `toast.gd` for individual toast UI
- Added toast calls for key events:
  -  Started (on START button)
  - Dispatching (when events are sent)
  -  Route connected (on sink click)
  - Delivered (correct box type)
  - Type mismatch (wrong box type)
  - Level Complete / 🏆 All Levels Complete
  -  Wrong destination (on failure)
  -  Restarting (on restart)

## Toast Types
- `ToastManager.toast()` - Default (gray)
- `ToastManager.success()` - Green
- `ToastManager.error()` - Red
- `ToastManager.info()` - Blue
- `ToastManager.warning()` - Orange

Closes #91

- :broom: Update or clean up current behavior

This PR adds defensive checks to prevent the application from crashing in multiple edge cases, including:

- Connecting a funnel (event source) directly to a sink.
- Attempting invalid connections after a valid Trigger → Sink path is already established.
- Starting the simulation with incomplete or partially configured event paths.
- Handling cases where conveyor and destination states become out of sync.
- Preventing runtime errors caused by null references, deleted event nodes, or out-of-bounds access.

The gameplay and learning flow remain unchanged the system now handles invalid user interactions gracefully instead of crashing.

pullRequests.title: Add sound effects issue #5
pullRequests.body:
## Summary
Integrates existing sound assets to enhance game user experience with audio feedback for all player interactions. (Fixes #5 )

## Changes Made

### New Files
- `Scripts/AudioManager.gd` - Singleton for centralized audio control with ducking

### Modified Files
- `Scripts/event_box.gd` - Added click sound when selecting source
- `Scripts/SinkClick.gd` - Added click sound when selecting destination
- `Scripts/ConveyerController.gd` - Added looping construction sound
- `Scripts/draggable_filter.gd` - Added poof sound when box is filtered
- `Scripts/level.gd` - Added success/fail sounds
- `project.godot` - Added AudioManager autoload

## Sound Effects Integrated

 **sfx_click_start.wav** - Plays when clicking source/box
 **sfx_click_end.wav** - Plays when clicking destination/sink
 **sfx_construction.wav** - Loops continuously after first belt created, auto-ducks when other sounds play
 **sfx_poof.wav** - Plays when event doesn't pass through filter
 **sfx_level_clear.wav** - Plays on level success
 **sfx_level_fail.wav** - Plays on level failure

## Technical Details

- **Audio Ducking**: Background construction sound automatically lowers volume (-20dB) when foreground sounds play, then restores to normal (-10dB)
- **Manual Looping**: Uses `finished` signal for reliable looping in Godot 4.x
- **Singleton Pattern**: AudioManager accessible globally via autoload
- **Smooth Transitions**: Tween-based volume changes for professional audio feel

## Testing

 All sounds play at appropriate trigger points
 Construction sound loops continuously throughout level
 Audio ducking works smoothly (background quiets for foreground)
 Construction sound stops on level completion/failure
 No audio clipping or overlap issues
 Tested across all game levels (BasicEventFlow, BoxClick, MultiSink, DlqPattern)
 No performance impact

## Demo Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cd07763-c2ad-42ee-b317-047871ee4ecd



Fixes #5

pullRequests.title: Add Outbox Pattern level with toggle-based failure/success flow (Issue #45)
pullRequests.body:
This PR implements a new educational level for the Outbox Pattern, resolving issue #45.

Changes included:

- Added a new scene: `Scenes/outboxPattern.tscn`
  - Introduces a visual "Outbox Table" (blue box)
  - Provides a toggle button to enable/disable the Outbox mechanism
  - Keeps UI consistent with existing levels (Start / Restart pattern)

- Added new logic: `Scripts/outboxPattern.gd`
  - Overrides default conveyor behavior for this level
  - When Outbox is OFF:
    - Events move toward the Sink and are lost (fade out), simulating data loss
  - When Outbox is ON:
    - Events are first routed to the Outbox node
    - After a short delay, they are reliably forwarded to the Sink
  - This allows players to directly compare failure vs. reliable delivery

- Updated `Scripts/level.gd`
  - Registered `outboxPattern` in the level rotation
  - Extended `sinkBoxMatchNeeded` and `dlsRequired` arrays accordingly

Implementation notes:
- All changes are scoped strictly to this issue.
- A new branch (`issue-45-outbox-pattern`) was created.
- Commits are DCO signed (`--signoff`).
- No unrelated files or behavior were modified.

The new level appears after the DLQ Pattern level and is ready for review and playtesting.

# Description
This PR implements a working prototype for the **Data Reference Pattern** (Address Issue #43).

### Demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/694a7566-1225-44ca-956e-68eee934591b



### Changes
- Created a new level scene: `DataRefLevel.tscn`.
- **Warehouse Logic:** Intercepts "BigData" payloads that are too large for the conveyor belt.
- **Token Logic:** Generates a lightweight "Claim Check Token" with smooth tween animations (Quad Easing).
- **Draggable Script:** Added offset calculation for smooth drag-and-drop mechanics.

### Why
This demonstrates how Knative handles large payloads by offloading them to an external store (Warehouse) and passing a reference token, solving the "Message too large" problem.

pullRequests.title: Dataref pattern
pullRequests.body:
### What I did
I added a new level to explain the **DataRef Pattern**.

### How it works:
- **Without DataRef (OFF)**: The data is too heavy (large payload), moves very slowly, and fails before reaching the destination.
- **With DataRef (ON)**: The heavy data is saved in a "Data Store" first. Then, a small "Reference" is sent quickly to the destination.

pullRequests.title: Dataref implementation
pullRequests.body:
This PR implements the core logic for the DataRef (Claim Check) Pattern level. It cleanly resolves the design requirements by introducing distinct success and failure flows based on whether the pattern is enabled.

Changes:

Implemented Failure Flow (off by default): Simulates network congestion with heavy payloads, causing the event to fail before processing.
Implemented Success Flow (DataRef Enabled):
Event moves to Data Store.
Turns into a lightweight Reference (Claim Check).
Moves quickly to the Sink.
Sink retrieves the actual data from the Store visually.
cleaned up unused files (
blo2E12.tmp
).
Optimized Scene resources by removing unused dependencies.
How to Test:

1.Open the DataRef Pattern level.
2.Press Start (without toggling): Observe the slow movement and failure.
3.Toggle local DataRef: ON and press Start: Observe the Claim Check flow and successful data retrieval.

Fixes: Resolves the implementation requirements for the DataRef Pattern level.
